description: List Jira sprints, or list issues in a sprint, with compact summary rows by default and raw Jira JSON optionally.
bundleDescription: Gateway-managed Jira CLI bundle for account discovery plus project, board, release, epic, sprint, and issue operations.

project.description: Optional Jira project key or id override for this call.

reporter.description: Optional reporter email or display name.

assignee.description: Optional assignee email or display name.

history.description: Whether to limit results to recently accessed issues.

watching.description: Whether to limit results to watched issues.

created.description: Optional created date filter, for example `today`, `-7d`, or `2026-03-01`.

createdAfter.description: Optional lower bound for created date.

updatedAfter.description: Optional lower bound for updated date.

createdBefore.description: Optional upper bound for created date.

updatedBefore.description: Optional upper bound for updated date.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 20 and must be between 1 and 100.

detail.description: Result detail level. `compact` returns summary rows only and is the default; `raw` returns upstream Jira JSON for narrow queries.

orderBy.description: Optional field used to order results.

reverse.description: Whether to reverse the default order.

paginate.description: Legacy zero-offset pagination string such as `0:50`. Offsets other than zero are rejected because `jira-cli` 1.7.x returns duplicate pages.

comments.description: Optional number of recent comments to include for `detail: "raw"` reads.

current.description: Whether to list issues in the current sprint.

previous.description: Whether to list issues in the previous sprint.

next.description: Whether to list issues in the next sprint.

state.description: Optional sprint states. Valid values are `future`, `active`, and `closed`.

showAllIssues.description: Whether to show sprint issues from all projects.

instructions: Use this tool to inspect sprints or the issues inside a current, previous, next, or explicit sprint. Keep the default compact mode for scanning and switch to `detail: "raw"` only for narrow reads.

examples.name: List active and closed sprints
examples.description: Lists sprint records for the current board context.

examples.name: List issues in the current sprint
examples.description: Lists issue records for the current active sprint.
